title: corbularia bulbocodium subsp. aurea
intro: Corbularia bulbocodium subsp. aurea, also known as the golden winter aconite, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Aconitum genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's roots and rhizomes are believed to possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate pain and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Corbularia bulbocodium subsp. aurea, such as tinctures and infusions, are used to treat various health issues, including colds, coughs, and respiratory problems, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity.
